published_prior_art.cite: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased — causal validity and fixed-window illness-death intervention substrate
MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased — EIF for its normalized natural-window intervention, not the displayed exogenous q_b intervention

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference — bounded-count diameter question for PN/PS ratios, not a CAR illness-death localization experiment

published_prior_art.cite: SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous gives n/delta effective-sample-size results for static continuous-exposure incremental effects, not a CAR multistate marked-Poisson limit

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference — honest-diameter frontier for weak-event PN/PS, not exact initiation-time survival risk

published_prior_art.cite: AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment and ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI do not give an honest exact-time CAR illness-death EIF frontier

novelty_flags.label: Definition EIF / Proposition 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The core treats Martinussen–Knudsen–Rytgaard Theorem 4.1 as an EIF formula admitting substitution of the externally fixed q_b, but that theorem conditions their Theorem 3.1 EIF for their specific natural-hazard-derived normalized-window intervention.

soundness_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 critical regime
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposed R-valued law Q_{P,lambda} is not attached to a defined centered/scaled statistic or triangular law P^(n), while P_n already denotes the empirical measure and a marked point process cannot itself converge to an R-valued law without a stated map.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Causal target and identification substrate
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Causal interpretation is assumed conditional on compatible full-data extensions satisfying consistency, observed-hazard causal validity, and CAR.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All claims about theta(P) as a causal exact-initiation risk are conditional on those extension restrictions; the statistical phase and honesty questions do not assert their empirical verification.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper defines its smoothed intervention by normalizing g(u)=alpha^o_01(u) exp{-A^o_01(u)} over a window, and Theorem 4.1 states the covariate EIF as psi_t(L)-psi_t plus the conditional version of its Theorem 3.1 expression.
The paper studies exponentially tilted stochastic interventions for static continuous exposures and gives bounds with effective sample size n/delta.
The paper establishes uniform Bahadur representations and continuous-treatment confidence-band theory for its minimum-variance-weighted local-linear estimator.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of Business & Economic Statistics 44(1), 2026, pp. 67–79
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper gives kernel-localized doubly robust cross-fitted DML inference for average continuous-treatment response and partial-effect targets under high-level nuisance conditions.
The paper derives minimax rates and a local-polynomial construction for Hölder-smooth CATE estimation.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the claimed q_b substitution by a separately derived EIF for the exogenous hazard a_b=q_b/Qbar_b, state its nuisance arguments explicitly, and retain Martinussen–Knudsen–Rytgaard only as informed-by prior art.

Define a triangular law sequence P^(n), the exact centered/scaled one-step statistic, and a Laplace-functional formula mapping its limiting marked point process to Q_{P0,lambda}; then state which limit has an atom.
Turn the honesty question into a boundary theorem candidate by naming a bandwidth order and a modulus/testing family that yields a concrete diameter rate or a non-Gaussian replacement interval.

recommended_next_step: Revise the EIF bridge and critical-regime object definition, then re-run novelty review; the repaired open kernel is field-grade rather than flagship-grade.
